09 - Monad Doors.Thebel (2-22) | Arqa (23-69) | Yabbashah:
You must fight Elizabeth alone.
No Nulling, Absorbing or Reflecting ANYTHING.
DO NOT use Armageddon unless it will defeat Elizabeth. Timing is key.
You must defeat Elizabeth in 50 Turns. If you take too long, Elizabeth will decide to end the show.
Breaking ANY of these rules results in Elizabeth summoning Pixe, casting Diarahan to heal herself to full, then say "I bring you Megidolaon!" and kill you, and repeat this turn after turn until you die.
Pixie will also appear after Elizabeth reaches certain thresholds in her HP total, which she will announce with voice lines.
If for some reason Enduring Soul or Endure become used before necessary, you will likely die and need to retry. Enduring Soul and Endure are essential in this fight.
Remember, timing is key.

Being properly equipped helps ensure you deal enough damage to defeat Elizabeth, while also having enough defenses to withstand Elizabeth's attacks.
It's not strictly necessary to have the best equipment, but it saves a lot of headaches. Also, DON'T wear any equipment that gives Enduring Soul or Endure.

Weapon
Lucifer's Blade - 425 Attack, and Increases all stats by 7 and grants Magic Ability, increasing non-Almighty damage by 25%, and can stack.
Available at Mayoido Antiques with Dead Moon's Husk, Ruby x10, Gold Quartz x10, and Fallen Angel Wing, obtainable from the Persona Lucifer. Recommended for an Elemental strategy.
Alternative: Excaliber - 300 Attack, and Increases SP by 100. Useful to not run out of SP too early. Found in a locked chest in the Adamah block.

Armor
Armor of Light - 400 Defense and gives +Reduce Magic Dmg (High). The absolute best at taking anything Elizabeth throws at you.
Available at Mayoido Antiques with Dead Moon's Husk, Diamond x10, Ruby x5, and Savior's Augite, obtainable from the Persona Messiah.
Alternatives: Fallen Noble Armor - 345 Defense and comes with Unshaken Will, which helps alleviate the status ailments Nebiros throws at you aside from 
Available at Mayoido Antiques with AS Refined Material, Gold Quartz x3, and Soul Sea Drop x2.

Footwear
Shoes of Light - 135 Evasion and comes with Ali Dancer, which helps evade a lot of Elizabeth's attacks. A great damage mitigator.
Available at Mayoido Antiques with Dead Moon's Husk, Diamond x10, Ruby x5, and Savior's Augite, obtainable from the Persona Messiah.
Remember you can register, dismiss, re-fuse as necessary to get enough Heart Items.
Alternative: Shoes of Bane - 90 Evasion and comes with +Null Dark instakill. This helps negate the skill Die for Me! while also ensuring you remain within Elizabeth's rules.
Available only through Tanaka's Amazing Commodities on 11/29 for 39,800.

Accessory
Divine Pillar - This gives Firm Stance, which halves all incoming damage but prevents dodging, so it doesn't synergize well with Shoes of Light.
If you're going to get hit, mitigate that damage! Reward for Elizabeth's Request #100.
Alternatives: Amritite Necklace - This negates all ailments, ensuring that any of Nebiro's status ailments fail against you. Availabe at Club Escapade for 500,000.
Incense Box gives +Insta-Heal which is almost the same thing, ensuring you're status-free when your turn comes up. Beware of lucky crits, though. Reward for Elizabeth's Request #97.
Book of Enoch - This gives +Magic Evasion (High). If you're wearing Shoes of Bane but want to try to cheese Elizabeth's Phase Two, maybe try these. Explanation will be later.
Available at Mayoido Antiques with AS Refined Material, Onyx x20 and Contract Angel Wing, obtainable from the Persona Metatron.

Items
Soma - The fight will wear you down as this fight takes a good while, so having a full HP/SP restore will be necessary.
Precious Eggs are good alternatives if you can afford a turn healing yourself after getting your SP back to full, or Beads if you just need to restore your HP.
Homunculus - These aren't necessary if you run Shoes of Bane, but if you don't, these are 100% necessary to avoid dying from Alice's Die for Me!
You can stock up at Mayoido's Antiques for Onyx x5 each. As you have 50 turns to win, you shouldn't need more than 10 unless you're extremely unlucky.

The basic strategy against Elizabeth relies on having the proper Personas to survive Elizabeth's attacks and stay within her rules.
You will need a number of Personas to fulfill roles in the fight, between 3-5 depending on skills and equipment.
Almost all high-level Personas have at least Null/Drain/Repel affinity, aside from Orpheus Telos and Chi You.
However, you can run lower-level Personas to fulfill roles as long as their stats aren't terrible. Remember, Incense Cards can be spent on one Persona, then registered to the Compendium.
If you fuse that Incense-fed Persona, the stats will pass down. Thus, you can spend time grinding money to fuse high-stat Personas.
Incense Cards can be purchased at Mayoido Antiques for gems, which can be bought from Club Escapade.
High Endurance is mandatory, while your damage dealer will also need High Strength or Magic depending on their skills. Agility helps with accuracy/evasion, and Luck helps avoid getting crit.

#1: Set-Up Persona - Not required, but helps out quite a bit. This Persona starts off the fight giving you every buff availiable.
Required Skills:
Auto-Tarukaja / Auto Mataru
Auto-Rakukaja / Auto-Maraku
Auto-Sukukaja / Auto-Masaku
Auto-Rebellion (If you want open with some Physical Skills)
Anything else doesn't matter, as you should swap out as the fight starts.

#2: Enduring Soul - You need this to survive the first Megidolaon from Pixie, when you trigger Phase 2. This can also be put on #1 to save space.
Required Skills:
Enduring Soul
You should also put on Concentrate or Charge to prepare for Phase 2.
Anything else doesn't matter, as you should swap out after taking the Megidolaon hit.

#3: Buffing & Debuffing - You need this to both ensure you are properly buffed and Elizabeth is debuffed. As the fight progresses, Elizabeth will be doing the same.
If you do not do this, you will be barely scratching Elizabeth while she wipes the floor with your face.
This Persona will be used repeatedly, so this Persona must have no Null/Drain/Repel affinities to avoid breaking any rules.
A high Endurance is extremely helpful to mitigate damage.

Required Skills:
Debilitate
Heat Riser
Insta-Heal (If you don't run Amritite Necklace/Incense Box)

Helpful Skills that aren't Required:
[Resist Skills]
[Regenerate 3]
[Invigorate 3]
[Spell Master]

Chi You and Yurlungur are recommended Personas

Decarabia, Hecatoncheires, Kaiwan, Ganga, Dakini, Robin Hood, Milady, Saki Mitama, Hanuman, Cendrillon, Loa, Oni, Johanna, Goemon, Legion, Narcissus, Zorro, Leanan Sidhe,
Take-Minakata, Carmen, Captain Kid, Naga, Rakshasa, Zouchouten, Berith, Slime, Valkyrie, Chimera, Onmoraki, Lilim, Forneus, Omoikane, Ara Mitama, Silki, Angel, Nekomata, Asparas, Pixie
and even Orpheus can work, although they are initially very low level for this fight and require Incense and levels to increase their stats to be useful.

#4: Almighty & Endure - You need this to both ensure you trigger Phase 3 by dealing enough damage at the right time, while also surviving the resulting unavoidable Megidolaon.
It only needs Magic to deal enough damage to trigger Phase 3. This can also be put on #3 to save space.

Required Skills:
Endure
Morning Star / Black Viper (Satan Only)

Helpful Skills that aren't Required:
[Almighty Boost]
[Almighty Amp] (Skill Mutation only)
[Multi-Target Boost] / [Single-Target Boost] (Satan Only)

#5 Orpheus Telos - The Persona that's basically built for this fight. It can inherit a wide variety of skills, has Resist affinity for everything, and has fairly high base stats.
As such, this will be your main damage dealer. You can instead use another Persona, but not having Resist for everything will make Elizabeth hurt that much harder.
High Endurance is mandatory, and High Magic or Strength depending on the build.

Builds:
There are a few ways to build Orpheus Telos, but the most reliable uses Ice Element. There are a few reasons for this.
1. You can hit Surt for great damage every rotation, especially in Phase 1. Other elements may have to deal with Elizabeth buffing/debuffing before the perfect moment.
2. Ice Amp and Ice Boost also affect the King and I Theurgy. This allows for massive damage against Surt when properly set up.

Element Build:
Recommended Elements are Ice and Wind. The example below will use Ice, but replace skills as appropriate for your preferred Element

Diamond Dust
Ice Amp
Ice Boost
Spell Master
Concentrate
Single-Target Boost
[Insta-Heal] (If not running Amritite Necklace/Incense Box)
Regenerate 3

Don't bother with Break Skills, as Elizabeth changes Personas too often for it to matter.

Almighty Build:
This is the simplest build, as you don't have to worry about Elizabeth's current resistances. This may be suitable for non-Merciless runs.

Morning Star
Almighty Boost
Spell Master
Concentrate
Multi-Target Boost
Regenerate 3
Invigorate 3
[Insta-Heal] (If not running Amritite Necklace/Incense Box)

Physical Build:
This is a high risk, high reward build. You can crit for massive damage, but it's dangerous since it uses HP to fuel Physical Skills.
It also may give an unlucky crit at the wrong time when you are setting up for Phase 3 and accidentally trigger it early.
Strike deals the most flat damage, so this build reflects that.

God's Hand
Strike Boost
Strike Amp
Arms Master
Charge
[Crit Rate Boost] or [Single-Target Boost]
[Crit Rate Amp] [or [Regenerate 2]
 Focus on Critting [Apt Pupil] or [Regenerate 3] Focus on Health

As a reminder: Have a Calculator handy, so you can react appropriately. Otherwise you will regret it. Subtract damage dealt to her HP to get her new total.
Endure and Enduring Soul are important skills that must be triggered intentionally. Each skill can only be triggered once.
It also helps to go to Club Escapade and having your fortune read. Make use of the Soul Fortune and have your Theurgy charge up faster.
Elizabeth has 20,000 HP.

Elizabeth always fights in a recognizable pattern. She summons her Personas in sequence, and uses their abilities twice in a single turn.
(Sometimes, Elizabeth will skip a Persona after taking an attack. Be careful!)

During the fight, you must attack Elizabeth with Heat Riser and Charge/Concentrate active in order to do as much damage as possible, while being mindful of her current resistances.
(Fuuka can help by scanning Elizabeth to see what her current resistances are. Don't forget!)

If Elizabeth has a Persona active that Nulls/Drains your attack, use the turn to either buff yourself or debuff Elizabeth.
Wait for your openings to deal damage.
It's recommended to Concentrate/Charge first, as those cannot be removed by Elizabeth.

One of Elizabeth's Personas, Alice, uses Die for Me!
This is the only instakill Skill she uses.
Keeping Enduring Soul and Endure unproc'd until the right time is essential to beating Elizabeth. However...
Enduring Soul and Endure proc before any Homunculus is used, which is why you need to place these skills on separate Personas and not have it on any Equipment
unless you equip Shoes of Bane, which Nulls Dark instakill skills but not Dark damaging skills.

It's important to note that certain Personas take extra damage from their opposing element, despite not having a Weakness. Make use of this.

This phase will trigger once Elizabeth falls below 13,000 HP.
(You can skip this phase by dealing enough damage to bring Elizabeth directly below 10,000 HP. Hit her hard!)

Elizabeth introduces this phase with the words "Magnificent!..." then summon Nebiros and cast Rakunda & Concentrate.
On her next turn, she will summon Pixie and say "I bring you Megidolaon!" dealing 9999 damage. No, that is not a typo.

To survive this, switch to a Persona with Enduring Soul. (Persona #2) Otherwise, you will die.
If possible, Concentrate/Charge on this turn so you can Debilitate Elizabeth to maximize your damage.

Now, you only have 3 turns to deal enough damage to bring Elizabeth below 10,000 HP.
During this phase, she summons Thor, and makes use of Thunder Reign, God's Hand, Tarunda and attacks you with the Compendium (Strike Attack).
(You can exploit this by using a Persona with a powerful Panta Rhei, so long as they don't have Null/Drain/Repel Electric or Strike affinities!)
Concentrate/Charge (or Debilitate if already charged/focused) then Heat Riser, then use your strongest skill and hope it's enough!

Trap Card Exploit?
There is a risky exploit that can skip the next phase.
If you Repel an attack that successfully brings her to below 10,000 HP, Elizabeth will immediately summon Pixie to kill you with Megidolaon for breaking her rules.
You can survive this if your Persona also has Endure.

However, she also does not restore her health with Diarahan!

You can use this brief opening to finish the fight with the Theurgy Armageddon.
If you do not, she will summon Pixie to heal herself with Diarahan and kill you with Megidolaon.

Try to use a Persona with poor Endurance that Repels Electric and Strike and either has Panta Rhei and high Magic to soften Elizabeth up or Rakunda to max out your Repel damage (And has Endure!)
Potential Personas are Mothman, Shiki-Ouji, Alilat and Mother Harlot which have one Repel each, so fusing/using a skill card to get the desired dual Repel affinities are necessary.
Elizabeth seems to open with God's Hand when entering Phase 2, so take that into consideration.

Once Elizabeth drops below 10,000 HP, she initiates the phase with the words "Marvelous..."

She then summons Pixie to fully heal herself with Diarahan and buff herself with Heat Riser.

Elizabeth then begins her next turn with Surt & Jack Frost, no longer sticking with one Persona each turn.
She instead will use 2 Personas each turn, each using a skill of their own, as previously listed.

Keep track of Elizabeth's last used Persona to identify her current affinities. Or ask Fuuka to do a scan; she's there to help you! Just don't tell Elizabeth.
The goal of this Phase is to get her as CLOSE to 10,000 HP without going under. Because once she goes under 10,000 HP, she will instantly summon Pixie and cast Megidolaon.

To counter this, you MUST attack Elizabeth with the Persona that has Endure, and bring her below 10,000 HP with that Persona active. (Persona #4)

If you do not do this, you will die.

Once she is below 10,000 HP and you are alive, you must immediately use the Theurgy Armageddon to end the fight. Otherwise you will die.
After the fight, you'll receive your reward, which proves that you have defeated Elizabeth. You will also complete Elizabeth's Request #101 and earn another reward.
